{"id":"project-LICHEN-gjtu","title":"EDHOC: Missing length validation before memcpy in multiple locations","description":"CORRECTNESS: Several memcpy calls have implicit length assumptions that could cause issues if constants are changed.

Locations:
1. Line 501: memcpy(ctx->g_y, g_y_ct2.value, EDHOC_X25519_KEY_LEN)
   - No explicit check that ctx->g_y has size >= EDHOC_X25519_KEY_LEN
   - Relies on struct definition matching constant

2. Line 864: memcpy(ctx->g_x, g_x.value, EDHOC_X25519_KEY_LEN)
   - Same issue for g_x

3. Lines 399-400, 788-789: memcpy(ctx->ed_seed/ed_pubkey, ...)
   - Assumes caller buffer is exactly 32 bytes

While these are currently safe due to struct definitions, they create maintenance hazards:
- If EDHOC_X25519_KEY_LEN is changed but struct fields aren't updated
- Static analysis tools may flag these as potential overflows

Fix: Add static_assert or explicit size validation:
BUILD_ASSERT(sizeof(ctx->g_y) >= EDHOC_X25519_KEY_LEN);

File: lichen/subsys/lichen/edhoc/edhoc.c:501,864,399-400,788-789","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:46:37.2828Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:46:37.282841Z"}
